title: Profile and fix a frame-time spike
description: Determine whether the frame is CPU- or GPU-bound and which stage dominates before optimizing.
body: Steps: 1. Measure CPU submission time and GPU frame time separately. 2. If CPU-bound, look at draw-call count and state changes; if GPU-bound, capture GPU timestamps per pass. 3. Find the dominant stage: vertex throughput, fragment/overdraw, bandwidth, or sync stalls. 4. Apply the matching fix (batching, LOD, reduce overdraw, lower-bandwidth formats, fix barriers causing stalls). 5. Re-measure against the frame budget. Rollback point: before changing render-target formats or resolution. Validation: the GPU timeline shows the targeted stage reduced and total frame time within budget; never optimize a stage that the profiler did not identify as the bottleneck.
